quizQuestion:
Necesitaba que yo {___} algo.
(She needed me to eat something.)
sentenceTranslation: She needed me to eat something.
explanation: comiera — imperfect subjunctive of comer (yo). After necesitaba que (past necessity), use imperfect subjunctive. -er pattern: comiera, comieras, comiera, comiéramos, comierais, comieran.

quizQuestion:
Si {___} menos, estaría más sano.
(If he ate less, he would be healthier.)
sentenceTranslation: If he ate less, he would be healthier.
explanation: comiera — imperfect subjunctive of comer (él/ella). Type 2 si-clause uses si + imperfect subjunctive. The él/ella form of comer is comiera.

quizQuestion:
Era importante que {___} cerca.
(It was important that we live nearby.)
sentenceTranslation: It was important that we live nearby.
explanation: viviéramos — imperfect subjunctive of vivir (nosotros). The nosotros form has a written accent: viviéramos. Note the double -i- pattern: viv-iera-mos.

quizQuestion:
Si {___} más, encontrarías trabajo.
(If you learned more, you would find work.)
sentenceTranslation: If you learned more, you would find work.
explanation: aprendieras — imperfect subjunctive of aprender (tú). Type 2 si-clause: si + imperfect subjunctive + conditional. The tú form of aprender is aprendieras.

quizQuestion:
Me pidió que {___} una carta.
(She asked me to write a letter.)
sentenceTranslation: She asked me to write a letter.
explanation: escribiera — imperfect subjunctive of escribir (yo). After me pidió que (past request), use the imperfect subjunctive. Pattern for -ir: escribiera, escribieras, escribiera...

quizQuestion:
Esperaba que {___} el libro antes de la clase.
(He hoped you would read the book before class.)
sentenceTranslation: He hoped you would read the book before class.
explanation: leyeras — imperfect subjunctive of leer (tú). Leer has a y-insertion: ley-era (like its preterite leyó). Pattern: leyera, leyeras, leyera, leyéramos, leyerais, leyeran.

quizQuestion:
Necesitaba que {___} pronto del trabajo.
(She needed you to leave work soon.)
sentenceTranslation: She needed you to leave work soon.
explanation: salieras — imperfect subjunctive of salir (tú). Salir is regular in the imperfect subjunctive (uses the preterite stem: sali-). Pattern: saliera, salieras, saliera, saliéramos, salierais, salieran.

quizQuestion:
Si {___} más rico, viajaría por el mundo.
(If I were richer, I would travel the world.)
sentenceTranslation: If I were richer, I would travel the world.
explanation: fuera — imperfect subjunctive of ser (yo). Note: ser and ir share the same imperfect subjunctive forms (fuera/fuese). Context distinguishes meaning. Type 2 si-clause: si + imperfect subj.

quizQuestion:
Ojalá {___} verdad.
(If only it were true.)
sentenceTranslation: If only it were true.
explanation: fuera — imperfect subjunctive of ser (él/ella). Ojalá + imperfect subjunctive expresses an unlikely wish. Ser and ir share imperfect subjunctive forms: fuera, fueras, fuera, fuéramos, fuerais, fueran.

quizQuestion:
Quería que {___} al médico.
(She wanted you to go to the doctor.)
sentenceTranslation: She wanted you to go to the doctor.
explanation: fueras — imperfect subjunctive of ir (tú). Ir uses the same forms as ser in the imperfect subjunctive. The tú form is fueras. After quería que (past volition), use imperfect subjunctive.

quizQuestion:
Si {___} dinero, compraría un coche.
(If I had money, I would buy a car.)
sentenceTranslation: If I had money, I would buy a car.
explanation: tuviera — imperfect subjunctive of tener (yo). Derived from the preterite 3pl: tuvieron → tuv- + -iera. Classic type 2 si-clause: si tuviera... + conditional. This is one of the most important B2 patterns.

quizQuestion:
Necesitaba que {___} más paciencia.
(She needed you to have more patience.)
sentenceTranslation: She needed you to have more patience.
explanation: tuvieras — imperfect subjunctive of tener (tú). The stem tuv- comes from the preterite (tuvieron). Tú form: tuvieras.

quizQuestion:
Quería que {___} los deberes.
(She wanted you to do the homework.)
sentenceTranslation: She wanted you to do the homework.
explanation: hicieras — imperfect subjunctive of hacer (tú). Stem from preterite: hicieron → hic- + -ieras. Note the spelling change c→c (stays) before -i-. Pattern: hiciera, hicieras, hiciera...

quizQuestion:
Si {___} buen tiempo, saldríamos.
(If the weather were good, we would go out.)
sentenceTranslation: If the weather were good, we would go out.
explanation: hiciera — imperfect subjunctive of hacer (él/ella). Type 2 si-clause with hacer as an impersonal weather verb. The stem hic- comes from the preterite hizo/hicieron.

quizQuestion:
Si {___} aquí, te ayudaría.
(If he were here, he would help you.)
sentenceTranslation: If he were here, he would help you.
explanation: estuviera — imperfect subjunctive of estar (él/ella). Stem from preterite: estuvieron → estuv- + -iera. Type 2 si-clause. Pattern: estuviera, estuvieras, estuviera, estuviéramos...

quizQuestion:
Me pidió que {___} la verdad.
(He asked me to tell the truth.)
sentenceTranslation: He asked me to tell the truth.
explanation: dijera — imperfect subjunctive of decir (yo). Stem from preterite: dijeron → dij- + -era. After me pidió que (past request), use imperfect subjunctive. Pattern: dijera, dijeras, dijera...

quizQuestion:
Ojalá {___} venir contigo.
(I wish I could come with you.)
sentenceTranslation: I wish I could come with you.
explanation: pudiera — imperfect subjunctive of poder (yo). Stem from preterite: pudieron → pud- + -iera. Ojalá + imperfect subjunctive expresses an unlikely/impossible wish. Pattern: pudiera, pudieras, pudiera...

quizQuestion:
Si {___} la respuesta, te la diría.
(If I knew the answer, I would tell you.)
sentenceTranslation: If I knew the answer, I would tell you.
explanation: supiera — imperfect subjunctive of saber (yo). Stem from preterite: supieron → sup- + -iera. Classic si-clause type 2. This is a key B2 structure showing hypothetical knowledge.

quizQuestion:
Quería que {___} a la fiesta.
(I wanted you to come to the party.)
sentenceTranslation: I wanted you to come to the party.
explanation: vinieras — imperfect subjunctive of venir (tú). Stem from preterite: vinieron → vin- + -ieras. After quería que (past volition), use imperfect subjunctive. Pattern: viniera, vinieras, viniera...

quizQuestion:
Ojalá {___} ayudar más.
(I wish he wanted to help more.)
sentenceTranslation: I wish he wanted to help more.
explanation: quisiera — imperfect subjunctive of querer (él/ella). Stem from preterite: quisieron → quis- + -iera. Ojalá + imperfect subjunctive for an unlikely wish. Note: quisiera also means 'I would like' (polite conditional).
